title: Air India Flight 171 Crash: Boeing 787 Disaster Kills 260 in Ahmedabad
dek: Air India Flight 171, a London-bound Dreamliner, went down seconds after takeoff from Ahmedabad on June 12, 2025, becoming the first fatal Boeing 787 crash and one of the deadliest aviation disasters of the decade.
body:
A Boeing 787 Dreamliner operating as Air India Flight 171 crashed within seconds of leaving the runway at Ahmedabad on June 12, 2025, killing 241 of the 242 people on board and a number of others on the ground, in a catastrophe that ended the Dreamliner's long record as the only major modern widebody never lost in a fatal accident. The twin-engine jet, bound for London Gatwick, struggled to climb after liftoff and came down in a built-up area close to the airport, where it struck buildings and ignited a fierce fire.

The scale of the loss made the Ahmedabad accident one of the worst involving an Indian carrier in decades and the first fatal hull loss of the Boeing 787 since the type entered service in 2011. A single passenger reportedly survived. The death toll, counting victims on the ground, climbed to roughly 260 as recovery teams worked through the wreckage at the impact site.

A failed climb after a routine departure

According to accounts of the sequence, the aircraft lifted off normally before losing the thrust needed to keep climbing. Within seconds it began to descend, unable to maintain altitude over a densely populated district near the airfield. The brevity of the flight, reportedly little more than half a minute from rotation to impact, left the crew almost no margin to diagnose or respond to whatever was unfolding.

Early scrutiny centered on the behavior of the engine fuel-control switches. A preliminary examination indicated that both switches moved from the run position to cutoff in the critical moments after takeoff, a finding that pointed investigators and the wider industry toward questions of how and why fuel was interrupted to both engines almost simultaneously. The cause of that movement, and whether any system or component failure preceded it, remained under formal investigation.

The first fatal loss of an aircraft type often reshapes how an entire fleet is operated and monitored, regardless of where the final inquiry ultimately assigns cause.

Pressure on a flagship widebody

The Dreamliner had carried a reputation as one of the safest widebodies in service, a composite-fuselage flagship that Boeing positioned at the heart of long-haul fleets worldwide. The Ahmedabad crash inevitably drew global attention to the type and to Boeing, already navigating intense regulatory and reputational pressure across its commercial programs.

For Air India, the disaster landed at a sensitive moment in the carrier's transformation under the Tata Group, which has been investing heavily in fleet renewal and service quality after taking the airline back into private ownership. The human toll, the international flight profile and the involvement of a marquee aircraft guaranteed that the response would extend well beyond India's borders.

What investigators sought to establish

  • Why the fuel-control switches reportedly moved to cutoff seconds after liftoff.
  • Whether any mechanical, electrical or systems fault preceded that movement.
  • What the cockpit voice and flight data recorders revealed about crew actions and aircraft behavior.
  • Whether any fleet-wide inspections or procedural changes were warranted for the 787.

What it means

The loss of Air India Flight 171 marks a watershed for the Boeing 787 program and for Indian aviation. As the formal inquiry proceeds, regulators, manufacturers and operators will weigh whether the findings point to a one-off chain of events or to vulnerabilities that demand broader action. For an industry that built much of its safety reputation on long accident-free stretches for its newest jets, the Ahmedabad tragedy is a stark reminder that catastrophic outcomes can still emerge in the most ordinary phase of flight. The coming months of investigation are likely to influence pilot procedures, maintenance scrutiny and public confidence in the Dreamliner for years to come.
heroImageAlt: Emergency responders at the smoke-filled crash site of an Air India Boeing 787 near Ahmedabad airport

metaDescription: Air India Flight 171, a London-bound Boeing 787, crashed seconds after takeoff from Ahmedabad on June 12, 2025, killing about 260 in the Dreamliner's first fatal crash.
